commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] This needs maintainer product/security review and contributor live-audio proof before any automated repair lane can safely take over.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a new network listener and shared HMAC secret path that need security hardening before merge.; Review finding: [P1] Bind the Teams media socket to an explicit safe host — extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.ts:149 - [P1] Make Tea... (sha=450015659240e6db6bf70cc22fa93f55103db6e5)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Microsoft Teams voice-call provider, HMAC-authenticated Teams bridge WebSocket transport, PCM TTS/STT wiring, config/schema entries, and provider/transport tests under the voice-call plugin.

PR surface: Source +1117, Tests +674. Total +1791 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: source inspection shows the unawaited media server startup, omitted listen host, missing runtime stop hook, and missing msteams validation/secret contract. Not applicable for an original bug reproduction because this PR adds a new provider capability.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs maintainer product/security review and contributor live-audio proof before any automated repair lane can safely take over.

target.title: feat(voice-call): add Microsoft Teams voice provider (OpenClawTeamsBridge)
target.body:
﻿## Summary

**What problem does this PR solve?**
- OpenClaw's `voice-call` extension supports Twilio, Telnyx, Plivo, and a mock provider, but has no way to handle **Microsoft Teams** calls. Teams audio cannot reach the existing webhook/media-stream plane because Teams calls are delivered through Graph Calling signalling + the `Microsoft.Skype.Bots.Media` AudioSocket, which only run on .NET/Windows.
- This adds a 5th voice-call provider, **`msteams`**, that bridges Teams call audio into the existing voice-call machinery (CallManager, realtime transcription, response generation, telephony TTS).

**Why does this matter now?**
- It unlocks real-time voice agents on Microsoft Teams using the same OpenClaw agent/STT/TTS stack already used for PSTN providers, with no changes to those providers.

**What is the intended outcome?**
- An operator can set `provider: "msteams"` and OpenClaw will accept per-call WebSocket connections from an external Windows worker ("OpenClawTeamsBridge" / AzureBot), authenticate them, and run the full inboundâ†’STTâ†’agentâ†’TTSâ†’outbound loop with barge-in â€” mirroring the streaming Twilio provider but speaking **PCM 16 kHz** instead of mu-law 8 kHz.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**
- The Windows-side worker (Graph Calling notification endpoint + `Skype.Bots.Media` AudioSocket) is a **separate component, not included in this repo**. This PR only adds the OpenClaw-side provider + WebSocket transport it connects to.
- Outbound dialing (initiating Teams calls) â€” the provider is inbound-only and throws on `initiateCall`.

**What does success look like?**
- `provider: "msteams"` binds an HMAC-authenticated WebSocket server; a live Teams call routed through the external worker produces a transcript, an agent reply, and synthesized speech back to the caller, with barge-in.

**What should reviewers focus on?**
- The **WebSocket auth/transport** in `msteams-media-stream.ts` (HMAC verification, replay window, connection bounds).
- The two **config validation behavior changes** for `msteams` in `config.ts` (see Risk).
- That the new provider reuses existing exported helpers (`generateVoiceResponse`, `CallManager.speak`, telephony TTS) rather than duplicating webhook logic.

---

## Linked context

**Which issue does this close?**

Closes # <!-- fill in if a tracking issue exists; otherwise remove -->

**Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?**

Related # <!-- e.g. prior Telnyx/Twilio realtime streaming PRs this mirrors -->

**Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?**

<!-- Note any maintainer/owner request here, or state "Community contribution." -->

---

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `voice-call` had no Microsoft Teams provider â€” `provider: "msteams"` was rejected as unsupported. This PR adds the provider, the HMAC-authenticated WebSocket transport, and the inboundâ†’STTâ†’agentâ†’TTSâ†’outbound pipeline.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.6.2 running as a local gateway on Windows Server (Azure VM); `voice-call` configured with `provider: "msteams"`; agent `microsoft-foundry/gpt-5.4`; ElevenLabs STT (`scribe_v2_realtime`, `pcm_16000`) + TTS; an external Windows worker placed real Microsoft Teams calls into the bridge.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `pnpm build`
  2. `node openclaw.mjs config validate`  â†’ prints `Config valid`
  3. `node openclaw.mjs gateway run --force`
  4. Placed a real Microsoft Teams call from a Teams client to the bot, then hung up.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): redacted gateway runtime log captured during the steps above:
  ```
  [gateway] http server listening (â€¦ msteams â€¦ voice-call)
  [msteams] starting provider (port 3978)
  [voice-call] Media streaming initialized
  [plugins] MsteamsMediaStream listening port=8443 path=/voice/msteams/stream
  [plugins] MsteamsMediaStream: connection open  <callId>
  [plugins] MsteamsMediaStream: connection closed <callId>
  ```
- Observed result after fix: the provider loads and binds the HMAC-authenticated WebSocket on `:8443`; a real Teams call completes the per-call WS upgrade (HMAC verified), `session.start` (callId / threadId / caller.aadId) is parsed and registered with `CallManager`, and `session.end` tears the call down cleanly. `config validate` passes with the full msteams + streaming + TTS config.
- What was not tested: the full bidirectional audio round-trip (caller speech â†’ transcript â†’ agent reply â†’ caller hears TTS) on a live call. That requires the external Windows worker to stream `audio.frame` messages over the WS, which was still being wired in that separate component. The OpenClaw-side audio pipeline (frame â†’ realtime transcription â†’ `generateVoiceResponse` â†’ telephony TTS â†’ paced 20 ms / 640-byte PCM frames) is implemented and unit-tested, but has not yet been exercised end-to-end by a real audio stream.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: end-to-end audio proof is gated on an out-of-repo component (the Teams Graph Calling + `Skype.Bots.Media` Windows worker). Handshake, auth, session lifecycle, and config are proven on a real setup; live audio is pending that worker.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before this patch, `provider: "msteams"` produced `Unsupported voice-call provider: msteams`.

---

## Tests and validation

**Which commands did you run?**
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.test.ts \
  extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.test.ts
node openclaw.mjs config validate
pnpm build      # clean compile of the changed extension into dist
```
Result:
```
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
```
`node openclaw.mjs config validate` â†’ `Config valid`.

**What regression coverage was added or updated?**
- `extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.test.ts` â€” provider lifecycle: session.start â†’ CallManager registration, transcript â†’ response, barge-in (`assistant.cancel`), playTts framing/pacing, hangup/teardown.
- `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.test.ts` â€” WebSocket server: HMAC upgrade verification (accept/reject), replay-window rejection, `session.start`/`session.end`/`audio.frame`/`ping` parsing, outbound `send`/`close`, and **inbound payload-cap rejection** (oversized frame â†’ close `1009`, added for GHSA-vw3h-q6xq-jjm5).

**What failed before this fix, if known?**
- `provider: "msteams"` was rejected as an unsupported voice-call provider; there was no transport for Teams audio.

**If no test was added, why not?** N/A â€” unit tests added for both the provider and the transport.

---

## Risk checklist

**Did user-visible behavior change?** `Yes (additive, opt-in)` â€” adds a new `msteams` provider option; existing Twilio/Telnyx/Plivo/mock behavior is unchanged. For msteams specifically, `inboundPolicy` now **defaults to `"open"`** (Teams is inbound-first), so msteams calls are accepted unless the operator sets an explicit policy.

**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `Yes` â€” adds `voice-call` config under `provider: "msteams"` (`msteams.{port,path,sharedSecret}`, plus `streaming` and `tts` blocks). Two `msteams`-scoped validation changes in `config.ts`:
1. `msteams` is exempt from the `fromNumber` requirement (it never places outbound PSTN calls).
2. `inboundPolicy` defaults to `"open"` for `msteams` when unset.
No data migration.

**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `Yes` â€” introduces a **WebSocket server** (operator-configured port) that accepts connections from the external worker.

**What is the highest-risk area?** The WebSocket transport (`msteams-media-stream.ts`) â€” it listens on a network port and accepts external connections carrying call audio.

**How is that risk mitigated?**
- Every HTTP upgrade is authenticated with **HMAC-SHA256** over `timestamp + callId` using a shared secret, compared in constant time (`safeEqualSecret`).
- **Replay protection:** upgrades whose timestamp drifts beyond a configurable window (default 60 s) are rejected.
- **DoS bounds:** pre-start timeout drops unauthenticated idle sockets; caps on total / pending / per-IP connections.
- Inbound message bodies are validated with a strict `zod` discriminated union.
- **Operator guidance:** bind the WS to a trusted network segment (e.g., Tailscale / private interface), not a public `0.0.0.0` surface. The shared secret must match the worker's and never be committed.

---

## Current review state

**What is the next action?**
- Maintainer review of the WebSocket auth/transport and the two msteams config-validation changes.

**What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?**
- **External proof:** end-to-end live-audio capture, pending the external Windows worker's `audio.frame` streaming.

**Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?**
- **GitHub Advanced Security (GHSA-vw3h-q6xq-jjm5):** the msteams WebSocket server now sets a 64 KB `maxPayload`, rejecting oversized inbound frames before parsing; added a regression test (oversized frame â†’ close `1009`).
- Internal worker/product names and phased-task references were removed from comments for upstream.

**Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?** None yet â€” initial submission.

---

## Files changed (feature scope)

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.ts` | New `msteams` provider: WS lifecycle wiring, inbound audio â†’ STT, response generation, TTS playback with paced PCM framing, barge-in, CallManager integration. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/providers/msteams.test.ts` | Provider unit tests. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.ts` | HMAC-authenticated WebSocket server; session.start/end/audio.frame/ping protocol; outbound send/close. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-media-stream.test.ts` | Transport unit tests. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams-tts.ts` | Teams PCM TTS helper. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/msteams.runtime.ts` | Resolves the realtime-transcription provider + telephony TTS and injects CallManager/response runtime for msteams. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/telephony-tts.ts` | Adds `synthesizePcmForTelephony` (raw PCM, for the 16 kHz Teams bridge), refactored out of the shared mu-law path. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/runtime.ts` | Hooks msteams streaming wiring into `createVoiceCallRuntime`. |
| `extensions/voice-call/src/config.ts` | `MsteamsConfigSchema` (`port`/`path`/`sharedSecret`); msteams exempt from `fromNumber`; `inboundPolicy` defaults to `"open"` for msteams. |
target.author: ahenawy

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
